factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong free cash flow generation, though there has been a slight decrease compared to the prior year. This cash flow is expected to support future growth and meet various cash requirements, including acquisitions and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Operating cash flow and access to capital markets are expected to satisfy our various cash flow requirements, including acquisitions and capital expenditures.
Free cash flow decreased $9.9 million over the prior year primarily due to lower cash flow provided by operations.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows from Discontinued Operations / Free Cash Flow

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated confidence in its stock's value through share repurchases, though there was no repurchase activity in the first half of 2016, contrasting with prior periods.

evidence.text: We use commercial paper borrowings for general corporate purposes, including the funding of acquisitions and the repurchase of our common stock.
During the six months ended June 30, 2016, the Company did not repurchase any shares of common stock compared to repurchases of $500.1 million for the same period in 2015.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is being managed through strategic financing activities, including the use of commercial paper and established lines of credit. The net debt to net capitalization ratio increased slightly, indicating moderate leverage.

evidence.text: Our net debt to net capitalization ratio increased to 42.1% at June 30, 2016 from 39.6% at December 31, 2015.
Commercial paper and notes payable, net increased in the 2016 period by $185.6 million, primarily to fund acquisitions.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is positive, as it is maintaining its current level of activities and expects steady investment in existing businesses and strategic acquisitions. This reflects confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Our ability to obtain debt financing at comparable risk-based interest rates is partly a function of our existing cash-flow-to-debt and debt-to-capitalization levels as well as our current credit standing.

Capital expenditures increased $0.9 million in 2016 as compared to the same period in 2015, primarily within the Fluids segment. We expect full year 2016 capital expenditures to approximate 2.3% of revenue.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different segments. While gross margins have slightly improved, operating margins have faced significant compression, particularly in the Energy and Fluids segments, due to factors like lower volumes, restructuring costs, and deal costs.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 20 basis points primarily due to increased productivity and manufacturing improvements as well as benefits from prior restructuring actions.
Operating margin declined from 11.2% to 0.0% in the Energy segment compared to the prior year quarter.
Operating margin decreased 670 basis points in Fluids due to lower volume, deal costs, and restructuring.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency fluctuations, economic conditions, and execution of acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our ability to obtain debt financing at comparable risk-based interest rates is partly a function of our existing cash-flow-to-debt and debt-to-capitalization levels as well as our current credit standing.
Foreign currency translation had a significant impact on revenue and margins. Economic conditions, particularly in oil and gas markets, are affecting demand.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

factors.summary: The economic conditions present mixed influences across different segments. While some areas benefit from expansion, others, particularly in the oil and gas markets, face challenges due to reduced demand and revenue declines.

evidence.text: We have considered the economic environments in which our businesses operate, particularly within those reporting units exposed to the decline in oil and gas markets, and the long-term outlook for those businesses.
Weakness in U.S. oil and gas-related end markets has led to reduced demand and revenue declines in the Energy and Fluids segments.
Overall economic environment is a tailwind for some segments but a headwind for others, particularly in oil and gas markets.

factors.summary: Management has provided mixed forward guidance, indicating both confidence in cash flow generation and challenges in meeting revenue and EPS targets due to weak market conditions.

evidence.text: Operating cash flow and access to capital markets are expected to satisfy our various cash flow requirements, including acquisitions and capital expenditures.
We have lowered our full year revenue growth expectations and our full year EPS guidance. [...] Full year EPS is expected to be in the range of $3.35 - $3.45.
Based on historical performance, we are targeting to generate free cash flow of approximately 11% of revenue for the full year 2016.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows from Discontinued Operations / Free Cash Flow

evidence.text: Our ability to obtain debt financing at comparable risk-based interest rates is partly a function of our existing cash-flow-to-debt and debt-to-capitalization levels as well as our current credit standing.
No specific discussion of interest rate effects on borrowing costs or demand.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed pricing power performance. While there is evidence of favorable customer pricing contributing positively to revenue, there is also indication of pricing pressure impacting revenue negatively.

evidence.text: Customer pricing unfavorably impacted revenue by approximately 1.8% in the second quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 0.3% in the second quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 0.4% for the six months ended June 30, 2016.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

factors.summary: The backlog orders are mixed across segments. While the Energy segment experienced a significant decrease of 33.3%, other segments like Refrigeration & Food Equipment showed varied performance with some decreases and increases.

evidence.text: Backlog was 129,873 as of the latest quarter, down from 194,819 in the prior year period.

Backlog for Printing & Identification was $104,509, up 1.1%, and Industrials was $210,646, down 15.3%, resulting in a total backlog of $315,155, down 10.5%. Bookings for Refrigeration & Food Equipment decreased 3.7%, while Fluids bookings increased 24.0%. Book-to-bill ratios varied, with some segments showing normal seasonality.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting with mixed results across segments. While the Energy segment is experiencing a significant decline in margins, the Fluids segment is showing growth and improved margins despite challenges in certain end markets.

evidence.text: Operating margin declined from 11.2% to 0.0% in the Energy segment compared to the prior year quarter.
Fluid Transfer revenue increased 26.3%, driven by acquisitions, while Pumps revenue grew 1.7%. Segment EBITDA margin improved in Fluids despite challenges in oil and gas markets.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with strong growth in Printing & Identification offset by softness in Industrials and decelerating demand in Energy and Fluids segments due to U.S. oil and gas market weakness.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was primarily driven by our Energy, and to a lesser extent, Fluids segments as a result of continued weakness in U.S. oil and gas-related end markets.

Printing & Identification revenue increased $33.7 million, or 14.7%, primarily driven by organic growth of 8.6% and acquisition-related growth of 8.2%, offset by the negative impact of foreign currency translation of 2.1%. Organic revenue growth was primarily driven by solid activity in our global marking and coding and digital printing equipment businesses.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

evidence.text: The decrease in Industrials platform revenue was primarily due to the impact of the disposition in the first quarter of 2016 of Texas Hydraulics of 7.1%, a decrease in organic revenue of 2.2%, and a minimal unfavorable impact of foreign currency translation of 0.2%. The organic revenue decline was primarily impacted by customer order timing in our environmental solutions business, and to a lesser extent, softness in select international markets.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has shown mixed performance across different segments. While Printing & Identification experienced strong growth, other segments like Industrials and Refrigeration & Food Equipment faced challenges, with some even showing declines due to market weaknesses and disposition impacts.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was 6.5% in the second quarter, driven by weaknesses in U.S. oil and gas markets.
Organic revenue growth in Printing & Identification was 8.6%. However, organic revenue decline in Industrials was 2.2%, and in Refrigeration & Food Equipment, it was driven by disposition impacts.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

factors.summary: Volume trends across the company show mixed performance. While the Energy and Fluids segment is experiencing a decline in unit volumes due to reduced customer activity and capital spending, other segments like Printing & Identification are showing strong growth. However, Refrigeration and Food Equipment segments are facing challenges with slight declines in volume.

evidence.text: Volume decline across the segment, due to the aforementioned market deterioration in the North American oil and gas markets, drove the overall decrease in revenue.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Volume growth in Printing & Identification was 1.8%, offset by unfavorable customer pricing. Refrigeration revenue decreased due to disposition impacts, while Food Equipment saw slight declines.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with significant slowdowns in Energy and Fluids segments due to weak oil and gas markets, while Engineered Systems and Refrigeration & Food Equipment show stable or slight growth. Additionally, there is strong activity in digital printing and marking, but this is offset by weakness in oil and gas markets affecting Fluid Transfer and Pumps.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was primarily driven by our Energy, and to a lesser extent, Fluids segments as a result of continued weakness in U.S. oil and gas-related end markets. This decline in organic revenue for our Energy and Fluids segments was partially offset by organic revenue growth of 2.0% and 1.1% in our Engineered Systems and Refrigeration & Food Equipment segments, respectively.
Strong activity in global marking and coding and digital printing equipment businesses. Weak oil and gas markets affected Fluid Transfer and Pumps.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2016 Compared to the Second Quarter 2015

factors.summary: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, with $9.2 million in charges in the second quarter and expectations for $40 million in total savings for 2016. Additionally, restructuring and productivity improvements are contributing to margin improvements in some segments.

evidence.text: We currently expect full year 2016 restructuring expenses of approximately $40.0 million. We expect to realize cost savings of approximately $95.0 million to $105.0 million in 2016 as a result of programs previously initiated as well as those enacted in 2016.
Productivity improvements and restructuring programs partially offset lower volumes and costs in Fluids and Refrigeration & Food Equipment.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaged in merger and acquisition (M&A) activity, particularly within the Fluids segment, with notable acquisitions such as Tokheim and Wayne Fueling Systems expected to close. These investments are aimed at driving future growth.

evidence.text: In addition to our acquisition of the dispenser and system businesses of Tokheim Group S.A.S [...] we also entered into an agreement to acquire Wayne Fueling Systems Ltd. [...] which we expect to close in the second half of the year.
During 2016, we deployed approximately $475.2 million, net, to acquire Tokheim, Fairbanks, and ProGauge within the Fluids segment.
